annotations.label: Cartan connection (informal)

anchor.snippet: a Cartan connection is a flexible generalization of the notion of an affine connection

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of Cartan connection; only a general `CovariantDerivative` on vector bundles exists.

annotations.label: Klein geometry

anchor.snippet: A Klein geometry consists of a Lie group G together with a Lie subgroup H of G

annotations.note: Lie groups and subgroups exist in Mathlib but the Klein-geometry packaging (G,H) is not defined.

annotations.label: Cartan geometry (informal)

anchor.snippet: A Cartan geometry extends the notion of a Klein geometry by attaching to each point of a manifold

annotations.note: No Cartan-geometry structure is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Affine connection from tangent planes

anchor.snippet: is an example of a Cartan connection called an affine connection

annotations.note: Cartan-style affine connections and rolling-tangent-planes constructions are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conformal connection from spheres

anchor.snippet: equips S with another type of Cartan connection called a conformal connection

annotations.note: No conformal Cartan connection is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Congruence of model spaces

anchor.snippet: A family of model spaces attached to each point of a surface S is called a congruence

annotations.note: No definition of congruence of model spaces in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cartan connection as generic G-connection

anchor.snippet: a Cartan connection on M is a G -connection which is generic with respect to a reduction to H

annotations.note: Reductions of structure groups and G-connections are not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine connection

anchor.snippet: An affine connection on a manifold M is a connection on the frame bundle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no frame bundle or affine connection in this principal-bundle sense; a separate general `CovariantDerivative` exists on vector bundles.

annotations.label: Principal H-bundle

anchor.snippet: a principal H -bundle is a fiber bundle P over M with a smooth action of H on P which is free and transitive on the fibers

annotations.note: General `FiberBundle` infrastructure exists but principal bundles with structure group action are not defined.

annotations.label: Principal H-connection

anchor.snippet: A principal H -connection on P is a 1-form

annotations.note: No principal connections are defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solder form

anchor.snippet: A solder form on a principal H -bundle P over M is an R n -valued 1-form

annotations.note: Solder forms are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cartan connection from connection and solder form

anchor.snippet: The pair ( ω , θ ) (a principal connection and a solder form) defines a 1-form η on P

annotations.note: Without principal connections or solder forms, this construction is unavailable.

annotations.label: Maurer-Cartan form of P

anchor.snippet: The Maurer-Cartan form η of P is the

annotations.note: No Maurer-Cartan form is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Structure equation for Maurer-Cartan form

anchor.snippet: η satisfies the structure (or structural ) equation

annotations.note: The Maurer-Cartan structure equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cartan geometry

anchor.snippet: A Cartan geometry is a generalization of a smooth Klein geometry, in which the structure equation is not assumed

annotations.note: Cartan geometry is not formalized.

annotations.label: H-related coordinate systems

anchor.snippet: Two such parametrized coordinate systems φ and φ′ are H -related if there is an element

annotations.note: The H-related coordinate-system relation is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cartan connection (infinitesimal symmetry)

anchor.snippet: A Cartan connection requires only that the derivative of

annotations.note: This infinitesimal-symmetry version of Cartan connection is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartan connection via gauge transitions

anchor.snippet: A Cartan connection consists

annotations.note: Gauge-transition formulation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Curvature (gauge formulation)

anchor.snippet: The curvature of a Cartan connection consists of a system of 2-forms defined on the charts

annotations.note: Curvature of a Cartan connection is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cartan connection via absolute parallelism

anchor.snippet: Then a Cartan connection

annotations.note: The absolute-parallelism formulation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartan condition

anchor.snippet: The last condition is sometimes called the Cartan condition

annotations.note: The Cartan condition is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Curvature of a Cartan connection

anchor.snippet: The curvature of a Cartan connection is the

annotations.note: Cartan-connection curvature is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartan connection as principal connection

anchor.snippet: Another way in which to define a Cartan connection is as a principal connection on a certain principal G -bundle

annotations.note: Principal-connection formulation requires principal bundles, which Mathlib lacks.

annotations.label: Cartan connection via Ehresmann connection

anchor.snippet: Yet another way to define a Cartan connection is with an Ehresmann connection

annotations.note: No Ehresmann connection is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Development of a curve

anchor.snippet: This curve is then called the development of the curve

annotations.note: Development of a curve via a Cartan connection is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reductive Cartan connection

anchor.snippet: is a reductive module for H , meaning that

annotations.note: Reductive Cartan connections are not formalized.

annotations.label: Parabolic Cartan geometry

anchor.snippet: is called a parabolic Cartan geometry , or simply a parabolic geometry

annotations.note: Parabolic geometries are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Examples of parabolic geometries

anchor.snippet: Parabolic geometries include many of those of interest in research and applications of Cartan connections

annotations.note: No parabolic-geometry examples are formalized.

annotations.label: Induced covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: Then the principal G -connection α on Q induces a covariant derivative on V

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `CovariantDerivative` on vector bundles abstractly, but does not derive it from a principal G-connection.

annotations.label: Leibniz rule for covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: satisfies the following Leibniz rule

annotations.note: The Leibniz rule is built into Mathlib's general `IsCovariantDerivativeOn` predicate, though not in the Cartan-connection context.

annotations.label: Canonical isomorphism of V-valued forms

anchor.snippet: In the presence of a Cartan connection, there is a canonical isomorphism

annotations.note: This isomorphism between equivariant forms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental (universal) derivative

anchor.snippet: This is called the fundamental or universal derivative, or fundamental D-operator

annotations.note: The fundamental D-operator from Cartan-geometry theory is not formalized.
